era: 1994-present. Riot Grrrl/Punk evolved into art rock, post-punk, garage rock. E=8 -- ferocious, intelligent, muscular, relentlessly inventive. Olympia to Portland.
associated_musicians: St. Vincent / Annie Clark (produced 'The Center Won't Hold' 2019)

theory_nodes.concept: ONE OF ROCK'S GREATEST BANDS. Time magazine 2001: 'America's best rock band.' Sustained critical consensus across four decades. 'Dig Me Out' (1997), 'The Woods' (2005), 'No Cities to Love' (2015) each named among best rock albums of their era. Feminist values and rock ambition not in tension.
theory_nodes.description: Sleater-Kinney as one of the most important rock bands in American music history, documented at extra depth.

theory_nodes.concept: Carrie Brownstein: guitar (mobile, rhythmically active), vocals (dry, ironic), Portlandia co-creator, author 'Hunger Makes Me a Modern Girl' (2015). Compositional intelligence through a guitar. One of rock's great guitarists.
theory_nodes.description: Brownstein as guitarist, vocalist, writer, and public intellectual.

theory_nodes.concept: Corin Tucker: one of rock's greatest voices. The vibrato: intense, wide, operatically present. Peak-range intensity. Heavier guitar register. Emotional delivery that communicates urgency without affectation. Feminist content in personal specificity.
theory_nodes.description: Tucker as vocalist and guitarist -- the emotional core of the band.

theory_nodes.concept: Janet Weiss: one of rock's great drummers. Technical excellence, physical commitment, compositional awareness. Fills low-end space in no-bassist arrangement. Quasi (with Sam Coomes). Departed 2019.
theory_nodes.description: Weiss as drummer and the structural role she plays in the no-bassist arrangement.

theory_nodes.concept: No-bassist arrangement: Tucker's heavier guitar + Brownstein's mobile guitar + Weiss's anchoring drums = complete sound without bass. Structural achievement of American rock. Two guitars cover harmonic and rhythmic ground of a four-piece band.
theory_nodes.description: The no-bassist structural choice and how it works.

theory_nodes.concept: 'The Woods' (2005, Sub Pop): THE MASTERPIECE. Dave Fridmann production. Loud, long, psychedelic. 'Entertain,' 'Let's Call It Love' (11 min), 'Modern Girl.' Consistently named best rock album of the 2000s and of the 21st century. Final album before 2006 hiatus.
theory_nodes.description: The Woods as Sleater-Kinney's definitive artistic statement.

theory_nodes.concept: Kill Rock Stars (276-277 continuity): Bikini Kill at 276, Sleater-Kinney at 277. Both on Kill Rock Stars. The riot grrrl lineage from Bikini Kill seeds movement; Sleater-Kinney transforms it into art rock. Sub Pop entry at 'The Woods' (2005) and 'No Cities to Love' (2015).
theory_nodes.description: Kill Rock Stars continuity and Sub Pop entry in Sleater-Kinney's label arc.

cross_references.relationship: Kill Rock Stars continuity. Riot grrrl seeds (Bikini Kill) to riot grrrl developed (Sleater-Kinney). Both Olympia. Both Kill Rock Stars. Kathleen Hanna and Corin Tucker: two ways to be a feminist rock vocalist in the Olympia tradition.

Olympia infrastructure. K Records (275) and Kill Rock Stars (276-277) as the two Olympia label poles. Evergreen State College world. Sleater-Kinney is the third Olympia entry in three consecutive degrees.
Produced 'The Woods' (2005). The production decision that created Sleater-Kinney's masterpiece. Fridmann's approach (Flaming Lips) applied to a punk trio produces something unprecedented.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=9 SPS maximum. Combined E=17. Multi-pod orca greeting ceremony -- the most emotionally powerful sound in cetacean biology -- paired with rock's most emotionally powerful Olympia-tradition band. Convergence and communal intensity in both registers.

milestones: SPS E=9 MAX TIED -- COMBINED E=17
